title: Immanuel
blocks: In the heart of Easter, I found myself inside a beautiful local church.
Although I wasn't particularly religious, I was drawn to the peaceful
atmosphere. As I listened to the sermon, something caught my attention.
The preacher was discussing the meaning of the name "Immanuel," which
translates to "God is with us." This struck a chord with me, as it was
also the name of the young man I had met on my first rainy cold night in
Rosario. The coincidence was not lost on me, and I felt a sense of
comfort in the idea that perhaps there was a higher power guiding me on

With these thoughts swirling in my mind, I wandered through Independence Park. My thought was broken by a voice, familiar yet unexpected, echoing, "Lee!" As I turned, my eyes met those of Immanuel, his face breaking into a grin, a vivid memory from our earlier encounter. By his side was a young woman, his girlfriend, her eyes radiating curiosity about the stranger her companion seemed to know.

The coincidence was divine. In a vast city like Rosario, the odds of our paths crossing again were slim. We delved into a conversation, the barriers of language challenging yet endearing as we flipped through an English-Spanish dictionary, just as we had when we first met, laughing at our clumsy translations. Yet the time, the ever-fleeting companion, seemed to rush as I noticed a hint of impatience in his girlfriend's demeanor. With a heavy heart, we promised another rendezvous, even as we acknowledged the erratic nature of our travels.
